findings.title: No Web Application Firewall detected
findings.description: The application does not appear to be protected by a WAF. This increases exposure to attacks like SQL injection, XSS, and other OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities. Recommendation: Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) such as Cloudflare, AWS WAF, or ModSecurity to filter malicious traffic.

findings.title: Missing security header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header. This allows MIME type sniffing attacks.

findings.title: Missing security header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Missing X-Frame-Options header. Application is vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.

findings.title: Missing security header: CSP
findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy header. This increases XSS and injection attack risks.

findings.title: Missing security header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Missing Referrer-Policy header. This may leak sensitive information via Referer header.

findings.title: Missing security header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Missing Permissions-Policy header. Browser features are not restricted.

findings.title: Missing security header: HSTS
findings.description: Missing HSTS header. This allows SSL stripping attacks.

findings.title: Information disclosure via Server header
findings.description: The Server header reveals Server technology and version: Apache. This information can help attackers identify known vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Missing or weak cache control directives
findings.description: Responses do not include proper cache control headers. Sensitive data may be cached by browsers or proxies.

findings.title: Missing Pragma: no-cache header
findings.description: The Pragma header is not set to no-cache, which may allow caching by HTTP/1.0 proxies.
